executive_summary: February revenue of $473,400 exceeded budget by $15,400 (+3.4%), driven by strong SaaS subscription growth that more than offset a $6,500 shortfall in professional services. Total expenses exceeded budget by $14,100 (+3.3%), primarily from legal costs and accelerated engineering contractor spend. Operating income of $31,300 beat plan by $1,300 (+4.3%) — revenue trajectory is healthy and expense overages are explainable.
attention_items: Professional services pipeline — $6,500 miss this month with two delayed projects signaling potential Q2 softness. Review pipeline with sales by March 15.
Engineering contractor spend — $6,500 over budget (+54%) with month-over-month increase of 95%. No confirmed roll-off date for the current engagement.

drivers: Subscription revenue of $412,800 exceeded budget by $17,800 (+4.5%), with month-over-month growth of $34,600 (+9.1%)
[VERIFY: Enterprise upsells and expansion activity] drove the beat — net dollar retention trending above plan
Logo churn was below budgeted levels, contributing to the positive variance
4100.context: Third consecutive month of positive subscription variance. MoM acceleration from $378,200 to $412,800 suggests the growth trajectory is strengthening.

drivers: Professional services revenue of $38,500 missed budget by $6,500 (-14.4%) and declined $2,700 from prior month
[VERIFY: Two implementation projects delayed due to customer-side resource constraints]
Backlog appears intact — revenue is deferred rather than lost
4200.context: Month-over-month decline of 6.6% after a stable January. If delays persist, Q2 services forecast should be adjusted.
4200.action_required: Review Q2 professional services pipeline with sales. Confirm delayed projects are rescheduled. Assess whether quarterly target needs revision.

drivers: Usage-based revenue of $22,100 exceeded budget by $4,100 (+22.8%) with strong MoM growth of $5,300 (+31.6%)
[VERIFY: Production workload adoption by existing customers] driving higher API call volumes
Usage pricing model performing as designed — revenue scales with adoption
4300.context: Largest percentage variance in the revenue mix. Monitor whether this growth rate is sustainable or driven by one-time customer onboarding.

drivers: Third-party API costs of $8,900 exceeded budget by $1,400 (+18.7%), correlated with the usage revenue beat
Variable cost rising in line with variable revenue — acceptable margin trade
Engineering salaries of $142,000 came in $6,000 below budget (-4.1%)
[VERIFY: Senior hire started mid-February rather than beginning of month], resulting in a half-month timing variance
6100.context: Expect this to normalize in March when the hire is on payroll for a full month.

drivers: Engineering contractor spend of $18,500 exceeded budget by $6,500 (+54.2%) — second consecutive month of increase (up from $9,500 in January, +94.7% MoM)
[VERIFY: Frontend contractor engaged for API v2 dashboard project]
6110.context: Month-over-month increase of $9,000 is the largest single MoM expense movement. If the engagement extends beyond March, this becomes a structural cost without a corresponding budget line.
6110.action_required: Confirm contractor roll-off date. If extension is needed, bring a budget revision proposal to leadership.

drivers: Paid advertising of $15,200 came in $4,800 below budget (-24.0%) and declined $3,500 from prior month
[VERIFY: Intentional pause on underperforming campaigns pending creative refresh]
6210.context: Favorable variance but worth monitoring — sustained underspend may indicate pipeline generation risk if not offset by organic or event-driven leads.

drivers: Events and sponsorships of $4,800 exceeded budget by $1,800 (+60.0%), up from $1,200 in January
[VERIFY: Unplanned fintech meetup sponsorship generating qualified leads]
Legal and professional fees of $11,200 exceeded budget by $5,200 (+86.7%) — nearly doubling from $5,800 in January
[VERIFY: Series B term sheet review and IP assignment cleanup] — expected to be non-recurring if financing closes on schedule
6310.context: Second-largest dollar variance in OpEx. Non-recurring classification depends on Series B timeline.

drivers: Travel and entertainment of $6,700 exceeded budget by $1,700 (+34.0%), up from $3,900 in January
[VERIFY: Unplanned customer meetings for renewal discussions — both renewals secured]
6400.context: ROI-positive spend if renewals are confirmed, but T&E trending above plan for two consecutive months.

drivers: Recruiting and hiring costs of $8,400 exceeded budget by $4,400 (+110.0%) — up from $2,100 in January (4x MoM increase)
[VERIFY: Agency fees for active searches — roles are budgeted but agency fee line was underestimated in annual plan]
6500.context: If remaining open roles also use agency placement, this line will continue to exceed budget throughout the hiring period.
6500.action_required: Evaluate recruiting approach for remaining open roles — renegotiate agency terms or shift to direct sourcing to bring costs in line.

cogs_summary: Total COGS of $89,700 exceeded budget by $4,200 (+4.9%), primarily driven by higher API costs correlated with the usage revenue beat. Gross margin at 81.1% remains within the 80-83% target band, down 0.3 points from budget but improving 0.7 points month-over-month.
opex_summary: Total OpEx of $352,400 exceeded budget by $9,900 (+2.9%). Favorable variances in engineering salaries (-$6,000) and advertising (-$4,800) partially offset unfavorable legal (+$5,200), contractor (+$6,500), and recruiting (+$4,400) spend. Net OpEx overage is manageable — the largest unfavorable items (legal, contractors) are expected to be non-recurring or time-bounded.

risks.exposure: $20,000+/month if Q2 projects do not reschedule and no replacement deals close
risks.trigger: Delayed projects not confirmed for March/April delivery by March 15

risks.exposure: $6,500/month ongoing if engagement is not capped or rolled off
risks.trigger: API v2 frontend project extends past March without a defined end date

risks.exposure: Approximately $15,000 additional annually at current 4% MoM growth rate vs. budgeted 2%
risks.trigger: Production workload growth continues without cost optimization measures

risks.exposure: $4,000/month above plan if agency placement continues at current rate
risks.trigger: Remaining open roles filled through agencies at current fee structure

recommended_actions.action: Review Q2 professional services pipeline — confirm rescheduled projects and assess whether quarterly revenue target needs adjustment
Confirm contractor roll-off date for API v2 frontend — if extension needed, prepare budget revision for leadership review
Audit AWS spend by workload tag and set monthly cost alert at 105% of budget
Decide recruiting approach for remaining open roles — renegotiate agency terms or shift to direct sourcing
